channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Under financial strain, primary care doctors unite
item.description: Under increasing financial stress, primary care practices across the U.S. are banding together in larger groups to negotiate more lucrative insurance contracts — and keep their clinical independence.
item.content:encoded: Under increasing financial stress, primary care practices across the U.S. are banding together in larger groups to negotiate more lucrative insurance contracts — and keep their clinical independence.
item.dc:creator: Karen Brown

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump hails Iran successes but offers no end date, Lebanon wants talks with Israel, and two teens are charged in NYC attack attempt.
item.content:encoded: Trump hails Iran successes but offers no end date, Lebanon wants talks with Israel, and two teens are charged in NYC attack attempt.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Trump hails Iran successes, doesn't give war's time frame
item.description: President Trump says the U.S. is 'achieving major strides' in Iran but doesn't cite an endpoint.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says the U.S. is 'achieving major strides' in Iran but doesn't cite an endpoint.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: How is Trump's war stance resonating with Republicans?
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks political consultant Sarah Longwell about President Trump's focus on Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks political consultant Sarah Longwell about President Trump's focus on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: With Noem out, North Carolina hopes to get FEMA funding
item.description: Politicians in North Carolina are hopeful new leadership at the Department of Homeland Security will result in finally getting FEMA recovery funding that's been delayed by Kristi Noem.
item.content:encoded: Politicians in North Carolina are hopeful new leadership at the Department of Homeland Security will result in finally getting FEMA recovery funding that's been delayed by Kristi Noem.
item.dc:creator: Gerard Albert III

item.title: Iraqi ex-foreign minister on regional fallout from Iran conflict
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel sits down with Iraq's former Foreign Minister Hoshyar Zebari to talk about the fallout from the US-Israel war against Iran, for Iraq and the region.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel sits down with Iraq's former Foreign Minister Hoshyar Zebari to talk about the fallout from the US-Israel war against Iran, for Iraq and the region.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Cyber warfare tactics increasingly used in Iran conflict
item.description: Cyber warfare is coming out of the shadows in the Iran war, from hacking phone apps to recruiting agents online to embracing AI as a weapon.
item.content:encoded: Cyber warfare is coming out of the shadows in the Iran war, from hacking phone apps to recruiting agents online to embracing AI as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Ukrainian towns turn to drone nets to try stop deaths
item.description: Towns in eastern Ukraine are putting up netting over sidewalks and roads to stop Russian drones from killing civilians and soldiers.
item.content:encoded: Towns in eastern Ukraine are putting up netting over sidewalks and roads to stop Russian drones from killing civilians and soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Guam objects to Trump administration plan to allow deep-sea mining in western Pacific
item.description: The Trump administration wants to allow deep-sea mining for critical minerals near the American territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands. Residents are worried about environmental impacts.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration wants to allow deep-sea mining for critical minerals near the American territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands. Residents are worried about environmental impacts.
item.dc:creator: Dana Williams

item.title: Scotland, Connecticut: The town with six ZIP codes
item.description: Scotland, Conn., can be a confusing place to live. The tiny town has six ZIP codes, which makes receiving mail an unwelcome adventure.
item.content:encoded: Scotland, Conn., can be a confusing place to live. The tiny town has six ZIP codes, which makes receiving mail an unwelcome adventure.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Brone
